content:
5 min read
Just now

--

I have never written a line of COBOL in production. I am putting that confession first, because everything that follows depends on you trusting me.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

I build backend systems in the stack people brag about. Postgres, Rust, Go, the languages that look good on conference slides and resumes.
And yet the technology I respect most is one I have never touched. A language older than my parents, written by people who are mostly retired or gone, quietly running civilization while we argue about state management.
Not a member? This friend link opens the full story free. The comments are open for your rebuttal.
Because here is the fact that should bother you far more than it does. COBOL has outlived every framework you ever went to war for in a code review.
Count Your Dead
Do the exercise honestly. Write down every framework you have passionately defended in your career.
Now mark the corpses. AngularJS, which owned the entire frontend and was then executed by its own creators.
Backbone. Knockout. Struts, GWT, and JSF if you survived enterprise Java.
Grunt lost to Gulp, which lost to Webpack, which is currently losing to whatever won…
